articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

images.name: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
images.caption: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Messi: It's a joy to continue at Inter Miami
articles.description: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

images.caption: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

articles.headline: Carlo Ancelotti speaks about life as Brazil's head coach
articles.description: Carlo Ancelotti is "having a great time" living in Brazil and managing the national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti is "having a great time" living in Brazil and managing the national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Argentina hand Brazil first-ever home loss in World Cup qualifying
article.description: Nicolas Otamendi scored with a towering header to give Argentina a 1-0 away win over Brazil in a bad-tempered World Cup qualifier that was delayed by half an hour on Tuesday after police clashed with fans at Maracana Stadium.
article.linkText: Argentina hand Brazil first-ever WCQ home loss

images.name: Argentina celeb vs. Brazil_20231121 [1296x729]

images.name: Is now the right time for Scaloni to step down from Argentina role?
images.caption: Gab Marcotti and Julien Laurens discuss Lionel Scaloni's comments that he might resign as Argentina coach after their win at Brazil.

article.story:
Nicolas Otamendi scored with a towering header to give Argentina a 1-0 away win over Brazil in a bad-tempered World Cup qualifier that was delayed by half an hour on Tuesday after police clashed with fans at Maracana Stadium.

It was Brazil's first-ever home loss in a World Cup qualifier, having entered the game 51-13-0 in 64 such matches.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Defender Otamendi scored from a Giovani Lo Celso corner in the 63rd minute with Argentina's only shot on target and Brazil's misery was compounded when midfielder Joelinton was sent off after a clash with Rodrigo De Paul in the 82nd minute.

The world champions handed their fiercest rivals a third consecutive loss as Brazil came into the match having conceded back-to-back defeats in World Cup qualifiers for the first time in their history.

Lionel Messi started in what was likely his last match in Brazil, but was substituted in the 78th minute amid a mixture of jeers and applause from Brazilian fans.

The eight-time Ballon d'Or winner wasn't able to end one of his last barriers in the sport at the Maracana, though, having now failed to score in all five World Cup qualifiers he has played against Brazil.

Argentina top the standings on 15 points with Brazil eight points behind in sixth place, the last spot that guarantees a berth at the 2026 finals.

Reuters and the Associated Press contributed to this report.
